inline_comments.message: Appc team asked nfs-provisioner not to be enabled by default.
Why disable this? If nfs-provisioner is enabled, there is no need in this pvc.

I think that Jerome was just trying to make APP-C and SDN-C consistent with each other.  I think the default should be whatever is the least amount of effort out of the box, but I don't have strong feelings either way.

I'm not sure about this change as well.  I've pulled this change down and tried it with the NFS Provisioner enabled.  I see two PV and two PVC per mysql instance, where I expected only one:

$ kubectl get pods -n onap

Exactly as James said, the idea there is to be consistent between sdnc and appc

the change I proposed was because in my test, when the provisioner was enabled, no pvc appeared and then by consequences no pv was provisonned. And when you look at it, a provisioner is not supposed to create a pv when nobody is requesting it. And the way to request a provisioner to create a pv is to present a pvc with a storageclass associated to that provisioner.
I agree, but appc team specifically asked to disable nfs-provisioner, which doesn't work over NFS, by default.

I brought that up with Arron on yesterday's integration call. I explained that the default will be to NOT have clustering out-of-the-box, and instead enable it via a production.yaml override file for all clustered components, he was in agreement.
For this to take effect, it also needs to be disabled in the top level onap/values.yaml.  Could you add this change to the review?
